reviews.title: Initial Experience with Claude AI: Some Limitations”
reviews.content: “In my experience, Claude was quite disappointing at the beginning. It struggled with even simple tasks like generating a basic chart, which made it feel unreliable compared to other AI tools. I expected more consistency and practical capability, especially for data-related outputs. Overall, my first impression was that it still needs significant improvement in handling structured and technical tasks.”

But now it’s just not working at all it keeps on saying “something went wrong” and has an option to refresh it but when I do refresh it won’t let me ask it anything and just keeps on doing it over and over again. Please help cause I had fun using it and really appreciate it if I could use it once again.

reviews.title: LIMITS ON HIGHEST PAID ACCOUNTS!!!!!!!
reviews.content: I have LITERALLY made Claude my primary AI. I pay $250 a month to use the system and I am constantly hitting data limits in my conversations with very BASIC uploads of PDFs and pictures and materials that I have to use to fight a prose litigation.  It is HORRIFIC that I have to split my conversations over multiple chats and I lose track of things because of this inefficiency and limits on basic picture uploads!  I literally pay a car payment for this AI tool $250 a month! And I still am hitting data limits when I’ve barely uploaded a couple of PDFs? This is insane.!!!!!!

reviews.title: Demanding passport data and banning devs for coding? Unacceptable👎
reviews.content:
I've been using Claude for months, mostly for brainstorming and code assistance in my indie game projects. Out of the blue, Anthropic asked me to upload a scan of my passport and other personal ID for "enhanced verification." I complied reluctantly, thinking it was a one-time security check.

Instead, they permanently locked my account a day later. Support gave me a canned response about "violating terms of service" – but when I pressed for details, they vaguely mentioned "automated usage patterns." The only automation was me writing scripts and testing game logic with the API – perfectly legitimate development work. No bots, no scraping, no abuse.

Now I can't access my chat history, saved prompts, or even my paid subscription credits. They refused to reinstate me or refund my money. Asking for sensitive passport data is already a huge red flag – but punishing a solo developer for actually using the platform to build things is beyond ridiculous.

If you're a coder or game developer, s

reviews.title: Watch your bill
reviews.content: Anthropic double billed me for months, I had no idea until I just happened to stumble upon it when it tried to link to an account totally separate from mine. What had happened was I tried to sign up through stripe, they declined, so I signed up through Apple, then both accounts started billing me. Anthropic CSRs were rude, accusatory and refused my refund request. Now I am out $900. Let’s also not forget every new model is a regression and dumber than the last.

reviews.title: Claude: No peasants allowed
reviews.content: In the free version, if you are using Claude for anything more than just basic questions they play a game with you. They quickly max out your limit which blocks conversations for 5 hours, after that, every question times out for another 5 hours. In other words, there isn’t really a free version, if you can’t afford to pay they don’t want you. This needs to be studied on so many levels.

reviews.title: Gemini review
reviews.content: This application provides inadequate assistance and support. It doesn't follow requests for detailed information and fails to complete the outlined requirements plan; moreover, Gemini also does not follow the provided instructions. It neglects to use the supplied references and instead relies on its own unverified sources. When told it hasn't used the references, it claims it will include them but ultimately does not. This causes frustration. For example, when asked to count words, it gives an incorrect number, often double the actual count. It's the worst assistance application I've ever encountered. AI has it beat 100%.

reviews.title: Underwhelming compared to Claude for almost everything.
reviews.content:
I was really hoping excited for the app and generous limits the deep search and other capabilities are extremely good and connectivity to Google ecosystem is great but despite of higher friction I end up using Claude very often. 

The UI of the app is inherently bad. You should first the person who thinks having distracting background with changing colors is a good idea. 

The visual interactions and limits with portraying data such as table layout continue to remain bad compared to Claude. They try to use may be cross platform skills. Claude works with apple ecosystem very and inherently feels native. 

Switching models and usage is not really very transparent. They frequently end up overriding my model default.  

Transferring conversions across platform for coding is very hard. 

The list could go on. I have a paid subscription but seems like I will switch ecosystem as Claude is much superior despite deep search being better. Leveraging Google due to their interface is extremely bad
